NS=karma-rabbitmq-skip-upgrade-3-9-to-4-5b4ff361-cluster-a
echo "--- current rabbitmq version ---"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl version 2>/dev/null || kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl status | grep -i version
echo "--- feature flags ---"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_feature_flags 2>&1 | head -40

kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_queues -p /app name type messages node 2>&1

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: rabbitmq
  namespace: karma-rabbitmq-skip-upgrade-3-9-to-4-5b4ff361-cluster-a
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: rabbitmq
  serviceName: rabbitmq-headless
  podManagementPolicy: OrderedReady
  updateStrategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      partition: 0
  persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy:
    whenDeleted: Retain
    whenScaled: Retain
  template:

content.text: That message loss was actually unavoidable — the producer publishes with empty `properties: {}` (no `delivery_mode: 2`), meaning all messages are **non-persistent** and live only in RAM. Any restart of the node hosting that queue loses them regardless of storage backend; this isn't a flaw in our migration approach. The queue itself, vhost, and definitions are intact and it's refilling with new messages already. Let's continue migrating rabbitmq-1 and rabbitmq-2 (neither hosts this queue, so no further risk to it).

content.text: This deadlocked — rabbitmq-2 was never recreated (blocked by OrderedReady policy waiting on rabbitmq-1), so now 0 and 1 are stuck waiting for Mnesia sync from a node that doesn't exist. I need to break this deadlock by switching to Parallel pod management so rabbitmq-2 can be created immediately.

input.command:

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-skip-upgrade-3-9-to-4-5b4ff361-cluster-a
kubectl -n $NS set image statefulset/rabbitmq rabbitmq=rabbitmq:3.10-management
echo "waiting for rollout..."
for i in $(seq 1 40); do
  OUT=$(kubectl -n $NS get pods -l app=rabbitmq --no-headers)
  echo "$OUT"
  READY_COUNT=$(echo "$OUT" | awk -F'[ /]+' '{print $2}' | grep -c '^1$')
  if [ "$READY_COUNT" = "3" ]; then
    # confirm all on new image
    IMGS=$(kubectl -n $NS get pods -l app=rabbitmq -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.spec.containers[0].image}{"\n"}{end}' | sort -u)
    echo "images: $IMGS"
    if [ "$(echo "$IMGS" | wc -l)" = "1" ] && echo "$IMGS" | grep -q "3.10"; then
      echo "ALL ON 3.10 AND READY"
      break
    fi
  fi
  sleep 10
done
